property_list_card.layout: Row based. Left: Square thumbnail (80x80, rounded 8px). Right: Stack (Headline for Price, Subhead for address, small badge for status).

comparison_card.layout: Vertical stack. Top: Thumbnail. Middle: Price/Name. Bottom: List of attributes (rooms, size, broker).

picker.style: Native iOS picker or Segmented Control for fast status changing.

comparison_view.layout_strategy: Horizontal scroll view (Carousel) displaying up to 2 items side-by-side on screen. Sticky top row with property image, price, and CTA. Scrollable attribute rows below with clear alternating row backgrounds for scannability.
comparison_view.attribute_rows: Address, Rooms/Size, Status, Rating, Broker, View Date.

layout_strategies.home_screen: Top Segmented Control (Buy vs Rent). Scrollable list of Property Cards below.
layout_strategies.detail_screen: Large Hero Image gallery at top. Scrollable content below. Key info (Price, Address) -> Status Pipeline selector -> Broker Info card -> Rating & Notes.
layout_strategies.add_property_screen: Grouped table view style form (like iOS Settings). Fields grouped logically: Core Details, Media, Broker Info, Status & Notes.

images.description: Modern house with pool (For Buy section mockups)
Modern minimal apartment (For Rent section mockups)
Wooden dining table apartment (Comparison mockup B)

instructions_to_main_agent: Use strictly React Native / Expo best practices for styling (StyleSheet or NativeWind if configured).
Adhere exclusively to the Light Theme values provided. Do not implement dark mode.
Ensure all interactive elements (buttons, segmented controls, cards) have an active opacity or touch feedback.
Implement 'data-testid' on all interactive elements. In React Native, use the `testID` prop (e.g., testID='buy-tab-button', testID='add-property-button').
For the comparison view, implement a horizontal ScrollView or FlatList where exactly 2 properties fit in the viewport width. Use sticky headers if possible, or keep the scroll simple and aligned.
Status pipeline changes should be effortless. On the detail screen, represent the status as a horizontal, scrollable tracker or a clear segmented control, not just a standard dropdown.
Do not use generic placeholder image URLs. Use the provided Unsplash/Pexels images as default fallbacks when a user hasn't added a photo yet.
Ensure input forms match the iOS grouped table view style (white cards on #F2F2F7 background with rounded corners).
All tab bars and headers must simulate iOS glassmorphism. Use Expo BlurView (`expo-blur`) where appropriate.
